automatedReasoningPolicySummaries.description: A list of Automated Reasoning policy summaries.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use in a subsequent request to retrieve the next page of results.
The token for the next set of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

modelDeploymentSummaries.description: A list of custom model deployment summaries.

nextToken.description: If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, use this token when making another request in the nextToken field to return the next batch of results.
Opaque continuation token of previous paginated response.
Continuation token from the previous response, for Amazon Bedrock to list the next set of results.

jobSummaries.description: A list of summaries of the evaluation jobs.

modelSummaries.description: A list of Amazon Bedrock foundation models.

guardrails.description: A list of objects, each of which contains details about a guardrail.

nextToken.description: If there are more results than were returned in the response, the response returns a nextToken that you can send in another ListGuardrails request to see the next batch of results.
If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, use this token when making another request in the nextToken field to return the next batch of results.

inferenceProfileSummaries.description: A list of information about each inference profile that you can use.

nextToken.description: If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, use this token when making another request in the nextToken field to return the next batch of results.
The token for the next set of results. Use this token to get the next set of results.
If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, use this token when making another request in the nextToken field to return the next batch of results.

modelCopyJobSummaries.description: A list of information about each model copy job.

nextToken.description: If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, use this token when making another request in the nextToken field to return the next batch of results.

If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, enter the token returned in the nextToken field in the response in this field to return the next batch of results.
If there are more results than can fit in the response, a nextToken is returned. Use the nextToken in a request to return the next batch of results.

invocationJobSummaries.description: A list of items, each of which contains a summary about a batch inference job.

nextToken.description: Specify the pagination token from a previous request to retrieve the next page of results.

If there are more results than the number you specified in the maxResults field, this value is returned. To see the next batch of results, include this value in the nextToken field in another list request.

provisionedModelSummaries.description: A list of summaries, one for each Provisioned Throughput in the response.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the policy.

definitionHash.description: The hash of the policy definition used as a concurrency token.

kmsKeyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the KMS key used to encrypt the automated reasoning policy and its associated artifacts. If a KMS key is not provided during the initial CreateAutomatedReasoningPolicyRequest, the kmsKeyArn won't be included in the GetAutomatedReasoningPolicyResponse.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the policy was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the policy was last updated.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy.

name.description: The name of the Automated Reasoning policy.

buildWorkflowId.description: The unique identifier of the build workflow.

annotations.description: The current set of annotations containing rules, variables, and types extracted from the source documents. These can be modified before finalizing the policy.

annotationSetHash.description: A hash value representing the current state of the annotations. This is used for optimistic concurrency control when updating annotations.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the annotations were last updated.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy.

buildWorkflowId.description: The unique identifier of the build workflow.

status.description: The current status of the build workflow (e.g., RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED).

buildWorkflowType.description: The type of build workflow being executed (e.g., DOCUMENT_INGESTION, POLICY_REPAIR).

documentName.description: The name of the source document used in the build workflow.

documentContentType.description: The content type of the source document (e.g., text/plain, application/pdf).

documentDescription.description: A detailed description of the document's content and how it should be used in the policy generation process.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the build workflow was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the build workflow was last updated.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy.

buildWorkflowId.description: The unique identifier of the build workflow.

buildWorkflowAssets.description: The requested build workflow asset. This is a union type that returns only one of the available asset types (logs, reports, or generated artifacts) based on the specific asset type requested in the API call.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy.

scenario.description: The next test scenario to validate, including the test expression and expected results.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the policy that contains the test.

testCase.description: The test details including the content, query, expected result, and metadata.

testResult.description: The test result containing validation findings, execution status, and detailed analysis.

modelArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with this model.

jobArn.description: Job Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with this model. For models that you create with the CreateCustomModel API operation, this is NULL.

baseModelArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the base model.

modelKmsKeyArn.description: The custom model is encrypted at rest using this key.

hyperParameters.description: Hyperparameter values associated with this model. For details on the format for different models, see Custom model hyperparameters.

outputDataConfig.description: Output data configuration associated with this custom model.

trainingMetrics.description: Contains training metrics from the job creation.

validationMetrics.description: The validation metrics from the job creation.

customizationConfig.description: The customization configuration for the custom model.

modelStatus.description:
The current status of the custom model. Possible values include:

  • Creating - The model is being created and validated.

  • Active - The model has been successfully created and is ready for use.

  • Failed - The model creation process failed. Check the failureMessage field for details.

failureMessage.description: A failure message for any issues that occurred when creating the custom model. This is included for only a failed CreateCustomModel operation.

customModelDeploymentArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the custom model deployment.

modelDeploymentName.description: The name of the custom model deployment.

modelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the custom model associated with this deployment.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the custom model deployment was created.

status.description:
The status of the custom model deployment. Possible values are:

  • CREATING - The deployment is being set up and prepared for inference.

  • ACTIVE - The deployment is ready and available for inference requests.

  • FAILED - The deployment failed to be created or became unavailable.

description.description: The description of the custom model deployment.

failureMessage.description: If the deployment status is FAILED, this field contains a message describing the failure reason.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the custom model deployment was last updated.

status.description: The current status of the evaluation job.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the evaluation job.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM service role used in the evaluation job.

customerEncryptionKeyId.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the customer managed encryption key specified when the evaluation job was created.

jobType.description: Specifies whether the evaluation job is automated or human-based.

applicationType.description: Specifies whether the evaluation job is for evaluating a model or evaluating a knowledge base (retrieval and response generation).

evaluationConfig.description: Contains the configuration details of either an automated or human-based evaluation job.

inferenceConfig.description: Contains the configuration details of the inference model used for the evaluation job.

outputDataConfig.description: Contains the configuration details of the Amazon S3 bucket for storing the results of the evaluation job.

creationTime.description: The time the evaluation job was created.

lastModifiedTime.description: The time the evaluation job was last modified.

failureMessages.description: A list of strings that specify why the evaluation job failed to create.

modelId.description: The model Id of the foundation model.

topicPolicy.description: The topic policy that was configured for the guardrail.

contentPolicy.description: The content policy that was configured for the guardrail.

wordPolicy.description: The word policy that was configured for the guardrail.

sensitiveInformationPolicy.description: The sensitive information policy that was configured for the guardrail.

contextualGroundingPolicy.description: The contextual grounding policy used in the guardrail.

automatedReasoningPolicy.description: The current Automated Reasoning policy configuration for the guardrail, if any is configured.

crossRegionDetails.description: Details about the system-defined guardrail profile that you're using with your guardrail, including the guardrail profile ID and Amazon Resource Name (ARN).

createdAt.description: The date and time at which the guardrail was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time at which the guardrail was updated.

statusReasons.description: Appears if the status is FAILED. A list of reasons for why the guardrail failed to be created, updated, versioned, or deleted.

failureRecommendations.description: Appears if the status of the guardrail is FAILED. A list of recommendations to carry out before retrying the request.

blockedInputMessaging.description: The message that the guardrail returns when it blocks a prompt.

blockedOutputsMessaging.description: The message that the guardrail returns when it blocks a model response.

kmsKeyArn.description: The ARN of the KMS key that encrypts the guardrail.

modelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with this imported model.

jobName.description: Job name associated with the imported model.

jobArn.description: Job Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with the imported model.

modelDataSource.description: The data source for this imported model.

modelKmsKeyArn.description: The imported model is encrypted at rest using this key.

instructSupported.description: Specifies if the imported model supports converse.

customModelUnits.description: Information about the hardware utilization for a single copy of the model.

createdAt.description: The time at which the inference profile was created.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the inference profile was last updated.

inferenceProfileArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the inference profile.

models.description: A list of information about each model in the inference profile.

inferenceProfileId.description: The unique identifier of the inference profile.

status.description: The status of the inference profile. ACTIVE means that the inference profile is ready to be used.

type.description:
The type of the inference profile. The following types are possible:

  • SYSTEM_DEFINED – The inference profile is defined by Amazon Bedrock. You can route inference requests across regions with these inference profiles.

  • APPLICATION – The inference profile was created by a user. This type of inference profile can track metrics and costs when invoking the model in it. The inference profile may route requests to one or multiple regions.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the model copy job.

status.description: The status of the model copy job.

creationTime.description: The time at which the model copy job was created.

targetModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the copied model.

sourceAccountId.description: The unique identifier of the account that the model being copied originated from.

sourceModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the original model being copied.

targetModelKmsKeyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the KMS key encrypting the copied model.

targetModelTags.description: The tags associated with the copied model.

failureMessage.description: An error message for why the model copy job failed.

sourceModelName.description: The name of the original model being copied.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the customization job.

outputModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the output model.

clientRequestToken.description: The token that you specified in the CreateCustomizationJob request.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role.

status.description: The status of the job. A successful job transitions from in-progress to completed when the output model is ready to use. If the job failed, the failure message contains information about why the job failed.

statusDetails.description: For a Distillation job, the details about the statuses of the sub-tasks of the customization job.

lastModifiedTime.description: Time that the resource was last modified.

endTime.description: Time that the resource transitioned to terminal state.

baseModelArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the base model.

hyperParameters.description: The hyperparameter values for the job. For details on the format for different models, see Custom model hyperparameters.

outputModelKmsKeyArn.description: The custom model is encrypted at rest using this key.

trainingMetrics.description: Contains training metrics from the job creation.

validationMetrics.description: The loss metric for each validator that you provided in the createjob request.

vpcConfig.description: VPC configuration for the custom model job.

customizationConfig.description: The customization configuration for the model customization job.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the import job.

importedModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the imported model.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role associated with this job.

modelDataSource.description: The data source for the imported model.

status.description: The status of the job. A successful job transitions from in-progress to completed when the imported model is ready to use. If the job failed, the failure message contains information about why the job failed.

failureMessage.description: Information about why the import job failed.

endTime.description: Time that the resource transitioned to terminal state.

vpcConfig.description: The Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) configuration of the import model job.

importedModelKmsKeyArn.description: The imported model is encrypted at rest using this key.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the batch inference job.

jobName.description: The name of the batch inference job.

modelId.description: The unique identifier of the foundation model used for model inference.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, case-sensitive identifier to ensure that the API request completes no more than one time. If this token matches a previous request, Amazon Bedrock ignores the request, but does not return an error. For more information, see Ensuring idempotency.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the service role with permissions to carry out and manage batch inference. You can use the console to create a default service role or follow the steps at Create a service role for batch inference.

status.description:
The status of the batch inference job.

The following statuses are possible:

  • Submitted – This job has been submitted to a queue for validation.

  • Validating – This job is being validated for the requirements described in Format and upload your batch inference data. The criteria include the following:

    • Your IAM service role has access to the Amazon S3 buckets containing your files.

    • Your files are .jsonl files and each individual record is a JSON object in the correct format. Note that validation doesn't check if the modelInput value matches the request body for the model.

    • Your files fulfill the requirements for file size and number of records. For more information, see Quotas for Amazon Bedrock.

  • Scheduled – This job has been validated and is now in a queue. The job will automatically start when it reaches its turn.

  • Expired – This job timed out because it was scheduled but didn't begin before the set timeout duration. Submit a new job request.

  • InProgress – This job has begun. You can start viewing the results in the output S3 location.

  • Completed – This job has successfully completed. View the output files in the output S3 location.

  • PartiallyCompleted – This job has partially completed. Not all of your records could be processed in time. View the output files in the output S3 location.

  • Failed – This job has failed. Check the failure message for any further details. For further assistance, reach out to the Amazon Web Services Support Center.

  • Stopped – This job was stopped by a user.

  • Stopping – This job is being stopped by a user.

message.description: If the batch inference job failed, this field contains a message describing why the job failed.

submitTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job was submitted.

lastModifiedTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job was last modified.

endTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job ended.

inputDataConfig.description: Details about the location of the input to the batch inference job.

outputDataConfig.description: Details about the location of the output of the batch inference job.

vpcConfig.description: The configuration of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) for the data in the batch inference job. For more information, see Protect batch inference jobs using a VPC.

timeoutDurationInHours.description: The number of hours after which batch inference job was set to time out.

jobExpirationTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job times or timed out.

modelUnits.description: The number of model units allocated to this Provisioned Throughput.

desiredModelUnits.description: The number of model units that was requested for this Provisioned Throughput.

provisionedModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Provisioned Throughput.

modelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the model associated with this Provisioned Throughput.

desiredModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the model requested to be associated to this Provisioned Throughput. This value differs from the modelArn if updating hasn't completed.

foundationModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the base model for which the Provisioned Throughput was created, or of the base model that the custom model for which the Provisioned Throughput was created was customized.

creationTime.description: The timestamp of the creation time for this Provisioned Throughput.

lastModifiedTime.description: The timestamp of the last time that this Provisioned Throughput was modified.

failureMessage.description: A failure message for any issues that occurred during creation, updating, or deletion of the Provisioned Throughput.

commitmentExpirationTime.description: The timestamp for when the commitment term for the Provisioned Throughput expires.

automatedReasoningPolicyBuildWorkflowSum.description: A list of build workflow summaries, each containing key information about a build workflow including its status and timestamps.

nextToken.description: A pagination token to use in subsequent requests to retrieve additional build workflows.

testCases.description: A list of tests for the specified policy.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use in a subsequent request to retrieve the next page of results.

testResults.description: A list of test results, each containing information about how the policy performed on specific test scenarios.

nextToken.description: A pagination token to use in subsequent requests to retrieve additional test results.

offers.description: List of the offers associated with the specified model.

tags.description: An array of the tags associated with this resource.
